app.name: kotoji
app.tagline: A home for the tools you build.

auth.breakGlassHint: Emergency break-glass admin login. Normally, sign in with Google.
auth.accessRestricted: Access to this instance is restricted. Your account isn't permitted to sign in. Please contact the administrator.
auth.notAuthorized: You don't have permission for this action
auth.setupSubtitle: Choose the admin password to finish setting up this instance. Once set, you can sign in with this account.
auth.passwordTooShort: Password must be at least {min} characters.
auth.setupError: Couldn't set the password. Please try again.
auth.setupAlreadyDone: Setup is already complete. Please sign in.

empty.title: No sites yet
empty.body: Create your first site and give your tools a home.

createSite.title: Create a new site
createSite.handleHelp: Lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens. Reserved words aren't allowed.
createSite.handleInvalid: Only lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens are allowed

files.title: Files
files.deleteConfirmBody: This will delete "{path}". You can restore it from history anytime.

empty.title: No files
empty.body: Upload a zip or create a new file to get started.

files.readOnlyOnPhone: Phones are read-only. Edit on a larger screen or ask your AI.

editor.leaveConfirm: You have unsaved changes. Leave anyway?

conflict.title: This change collided with another
conflict.body: While you were editing, someone (or an AI) updated this file. To stay safe, review before overwriting.
conflict.reloadHint: Recommended: load the server's latest version (your edits are discarded).
conflict.overwriteHint: Replace the server's changes with your edits.

empty.title: No alternate versions yet
empty.body: Create a new version to try things apart from your draft.

publish.title: Publish
publish.warning: Publishing goes live at {url} immediately. You can roll back later.
publish.confirmBody: This applies your draft changes to the published version. You can roll back later.
publish.conflictBody: What you tried to publish is out of date. Review the latest and try again.
publish.mergeConflictBody: A conflict occurred while publishing. Please review these files:

history.title: History
history.rollbackConfirmBody: Restores the content from this point. It's recorded as new history, so you can always redo.

empty.title: No history yet
empty.body: Your first save will show up here.

members.title: Members
members.cannotRemoveSoleOwner: You can't remove the sole owner

tokens.title: MCP / API tokens
tokens.description: Issue your own token to connect AI tools to kotoji. One token works across every project you're a member of (its access on each project is capped to what your role there allows).
tokens.name: Token name
tokens.expiresHint: Leave blank for a token that never expires.
tokens.canCreateSitesHint: Let this token create new projects via MCP. Only takes effect if you can create sites yourself.
tokens.revokeConfirmBody: This revokes "{name}". It can no longer connect.
tokens.showOnceBody: This token is shown only once and can't be shown again. Store it somewhere safe.

empty.title: No tokens yet
empty.body: Issue a token to connect with AI tools.

settings.title: Settings
settings.githubDescription: Link this site to a GitHub repository to mirror (force-push) the draft and published content.
settings.githubDisabledHint: GitHub mirroring is disabled on this instance. Ask an admin to set KOTOJI_GITHUB_* in .env to enable it.
settings.renameNote: Your old URL will redirect to the new one.
settings.deleteConfirmBody: This deletes "{handle}". It's recoverable for 30 days. Type the handle to confirm.
settings.instanceSubtitle: Settings for this instance and your account. Per-project settings live on each project's tabs.
settings.instanceGithubDescription: Instance-wide GitHub mirroring config. The token, org, and enable flag set here are used by every project's GitHub linking.
settings.instanceGithubEnableHint: When enabled, projects can mirror (force-push) to a GitHub repository.
settings.instanceGithubOrgHint: The GitHub org or user that owns created repositories. e.g. necorox-com
settings.instanceGithubOrgInvalid: Enter a valid GitHub org/user name (no symbols or spaces).
settings.instanceGithubTokenHint: A PAT with push access to the repositories. It is encrypted on save and never shown again.
settings.instanceGithubTokenUpdatePlaceholder: Enter a new value to update (leave blank to keep the current one)
settings.instanceGithubWebhookSecretHint: Secret used to verify GitHub webhook signatures (optional).
settings.instanceGithubNeedsToken: Mirroring needs an access token to push. Set a token to enable it.
settings.instanceDomainDescription: Instance-wide base domain and control URL. A field set via an environment variable is locked and read-only.
settings.instanceDomainBaseDomainHint: The domain used to serve projects, e.g. hosting.example.com. Projects are reached at {handle}.<base domain>.
settings.instanceDomainBaseDomainInvalid: Enter a valid hostname (no scheme, port, or path).
settings.instanceDomainControlBaseURLHint: The external URL of this instance's control panel, e.g. https://hosting.example.com. Used for absolute links and CORS.
settings.instanceDomainControlBaseURLInvalid: Enter a valid absolute http(s) URL (no path or fragment).
settings.instanceDomainSourceDerived: Auto-detected from the request (not set yet)
settings.instanceDomainLocked: Locked by an environment variable. Unset the variable to edit this field.
settings.instanceDomainLockedError: This field is locked by an environment variable and can't be changed.
settings.instanceDomainLockedTooltipBaseDomain: This value is set by the KOTOJI_BASE_DOMAIN environment variable and can't be edited here. Unset the variable to change it.
settings.instanceDomainLockedTooltipControlBaseUR: This value is set by the KOTOJI_CONTROL_BASE_URL environment variable and can't be edited here. Unset the variable to change it.
settings.instanceDomainDerivedHint: Not set yet — this value is currently derived from the request. Enter the real value and save.
settings.instanceDomainNoteDns: DNS records (your-domain and *.your-domain pointing to the server IP) and the TLS certificate are configured outside this app. Point DNS and your certificate at this domain.
settings.instanceDomainNoteCookie: Changing the control base URL may require signing in again, because the cookie domain changes.
settings.signinDescription: Enable and configure Google sign-in (OIDC). Fields set via an environment variable are locked and cannot be changed here.
settings.oidcConsoleNoteTitle: Set up in Google Cloud Console
settings.oidcConsoleNote: You still create one OAuth client in Google Cloud Console and register the "Redirect URI" below as an authorized redirect URI. Everything else is configured right here.
settings.oidcEnableHint: When enabled, a "Continue with Google" button appears on the login page. The emergency admin password always remains available.
settings.oidcIssuerHint: The OIDC provider's issuer URL. For Google, leave it as https://accounts.google.com.
settings.oidcIssuerInvalid: Enter a valid absolute http(s) URL.
settings.oidcClientIdHint: The OAuth client ID issued in Google Cloud Console (not a secret).
settings.oidcClientSecretHint: The OAuth client secret. Write-only and stored encrypted at rest. Leave blank to keep the existing value.
settings.oidcSecretUpdatePlaceholder: Enter a new value only to change it
settings.oidcRedirectUrlNone: (auto-generated once a control base URL is set)
settings.oidcRedirectUrlHintConfigured: Using an explicitly-configured redirect URI. Register this value in Google Cloud Console.
settings.oidcRedirectUrlHintDerived: Auto-derived from the control base URL + /auth/callback. Register this value in Google Cloud Console.
settings.oidcRedirectUrlOverrideHint: Usually leave this blank. Enter an absolute http(s) URL only if you need to override the auto-derived value.
settings.oidcRedirectUrlInvalid: Enter a valid absolute http(s) URL.
settings.oidcAllowedEmailsHint: Exact email addresses allowed to sign in. Separate multiple entries with newlines or commas.
settings.oidcAllowedDomainsHint: Email domains allowed to sign in (e.g. example.com). Separate multiple entries with newlines or commas.
settings.oidcAdminEmailsHint: Users who sign in with one of these emails are automatically granted admin. Separate multiple entries with newlines or commas.
settings.oidcFailClosedRule: Enabling OIDC requires a client ID and client secret, plus at least one of "Allowed emails" or "Allowed domains" (fail-closed: empty allowlists deny all sign-ins).
settings.oidcBreakGlassNote: Enabling OIDC never removes the emergency admin-password sign-in.
settings.oidcLockedError: This field is locked by an environment variable and cannot be changed.
settings.oidcLockedTooltipAuthMode: The auth provider set is pinned by the KOTOJI_AUTH_MODE environment variable and cannot be changed here.
settings.oidcLockedTooltipEnabled: This value is set via an environment variable and cannot be changed here. Unset the variable to edit it.
settings.oidcLockedTooltipIssuer: This value is set via the KOTOJI_OIDC_ISSUER environment variable and cannot be changed here.
settings.oidcLockedTooltipClientId: This value is set via the KOTOJI_OIDC_CLIENT_ID environment variable and cannot be changed here.
settings.oidcLockedTooltipClientSecret: This value is set via the KOTOJI_OIDC_CLIENT_SECRET environment variable and cannot be changed here.
settings.oidcLockedTooltipRedirectUrl: This value is set via the KOTOJI_OIDC_REDIRECT_URL environment variable and cannot be changed here.
settings.oidcLockedTooltipAllowedEmails: This value is set via the KOTOJI_OIDC_ALLOWED_EMAILS environment variable and cannot be changed here.
settings.oidcLockedTooltipAllowedDomains: This value is set via the KOTOJI_OIDC_ALLOWED_DOMAINS environment variable and cannot be changed here.
settings.oidcLockedTooltipAdminEmails: This value is set via the KOTOJI_OIDC_ADMIN_EMAILS environment variable and cannot be changed here.
settings.mcpGuideDescription: How to connect an AI tool such as Claude to this instance.
settings.mcpEndpointBody: Point your AI client at this endpoint URL.
settings.mcpTokenBody: Connecting needs one access token of your own. Issue it from the "MCP / API tokens" section at the top of this page — a single token works across every project you're a member of. <link>Issue a token</link>.
settings.mcpCliBody: Run this command in your terminal (replace kotoji_pat_… with the token you issued).
settings.mcpDesktopBody: Add this entry under mcpServers in your Claude Desktop config file.
settings.mcpToolsBody: Once connected, your AI assistant can perform these actions. Every tool except list_sites and create_site takes a site argument (the project handle); you can only act on projects you're a member of.
settings.mcpCloudflareBody: When this instance is behind a Cloudflare proxy, Bot Fight Mode may block requests to /mcp. If so, add a WAF skip (exception rule) for the /mcp path.
settings.tlsDescription: How HTTPS is terminated for this instance. kotoji can either sit behind your own edge or manage certificates itself.
settings.tlsModeOffTitle: TLS handled by your proxy / the Traefik overlay
settings.tlsModeOffBody: By default kotoji speaks plain HTTP on its ports and a reverse proxy in front of it (your own proxy, the Traefik overlay, or Cloudflare) terminates TLS. This is the default and what a Cloudflare-fronted deployment uses.
settings.tlsModeAutoBody: kotoji can terminate TLS itself: certificates are issued on demand for your sites the first time a request arrives for a host this instance can serve (Let's Encrypt, no wildcard, no DNS token). Unknown hosts are refused. Once you point DNS at the server, issuance and renewal are fully automatic.
settings.tlsModeAutoEnable: Enabling automatic HTTPS is a deploy choice: run the {overlay} overlay (it publishes ports 80 and 443 and sets {env}). It cannot be toggled from this screen because the listeners are bound at startup.
settings.tlsCoverageBody: In automatic mode, a certificate is obtained per host on demand: the control host below, plus every published site and preview host. Nothing to configure beyond DNS.
settings.tlsDocsHint: See the {link} for the full TLS / deploy-mode reference.

admin.title: Admin

comingSoon.title: Coming soon
comingSoon.body: This admin area isn't available yet.

admin.usersDescription: Manage instance users and their permissions.
admin.projectsDescription: Review every site's status, size, and updates.
admin.reservedWordsDescription: Manage handles that can't be used.

errors.rateLimited: Too many requests. Please wait a moment.
